verdicts.justification: Inverting the line-161 dedup compare makes any non-matching ledger entry 'match' first, so a fresh lesson is merged into an unrelated entry instead of appended — observable in the stdout JSON (action merged vs appended, wrong entry_line) and in LESSONS.md content.

Negating the line-97 guard flips when a Beads task is created: with bd and .beads present, `add ... --bd-task` yields bd_task_id null (and plain `add` creates a spurious task), observable in cmd_add's stdout JSON bd_task_id/observations and in bd state.

Inverting the line-421 circuit breaker makes every normal Stop (stop_hook_active=false) emit {} and exit before any gating, so any test that pipes a normal Stop payload with tracked changes and expects a {"decision":"block"} envelope distinguishes the mutant.

Deleting the `echo {}; exit 0` body lets a stop_hook_active=true invocation fall through into change detection, so with tracked changes the mutant emits a block decision on the hook's stdout where the original emits {} (the H3 re-entry allow).

Stubbing out the line-296 `bd label remove` means approve/choose never actually drop qa-gate-entered, qa-pending, or qa-escalated in Beads, observable via `bd show` labels, the status subcommand's rubric/qa readout, and verify-before-stop's task_has_label replay path after `choose continue`.

`! add_label ... || true` evaluates to status 0 whether add_label succeeds or fails, so a fresh `qa-gate.sh enter` always takes the error branch — emitting ok:false 'failed to add qa-gate-entered label' and exit 2 instead of the entered JSON, on the script's primary stdout/exit surface.

The line-99 printf is a bash builtin writing a fixed-format %s into merge_sources' command-substitution capture (read fully by the same shell), which has no constructible failure mode, so the masked exit status is never observable to any caller of lessons.sh.

The jq exit code is cmd_get_json's (and under set -e the script's) exit status, and a test can force jq invocation failure through the script's own data interface (a >ARG_MAX single-line current-task file gives E2BIG, exit 126 vs the mutant's 0) or a PATH-stubbed failing jq, distinguishing the mutant at the exit-code surface.

Without the $(pwd) default an unset CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR yields PROJECT_DIR="" so LEDGER_FILE resolves to /LESSONS.md, and the line-134 seed-file check makes `lessons.sh add` in a tempdir fail (exit 1, stderr error) where the original succeeds against the tempdir's ledger.

current-task.sh sets -e but never -u, so at line 77 ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} and ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-} both expand to the empty string when unset and to the same value when set, leaving the [ -n ] test and every downstream fingerprint consumer identical under any script invocation.

lessons.sh has no set -u, so ${1} and ${1:-} expand identically (empty when --source is the last arg, the value otherwise), and the subsequent [ -z "$source_id" ] usage check behaves the same — no consumer of cmd_add can observe a difference.

The trailing sed only trims leading/trailing [[:space:]], so a current-task file whose first line contains an interior CR (e.g. 'abcdef', writable directly since the file is cmd_get's input surface) makes `current-task.sh get` print 'abcdef' under the mutant vs 'abcdef' originally, a divergent task-id value consumed by the hooks and bd.

printf reuses a short format for leftover arguments, so the 5-slot mutant with 6 args appends two malformed rows ('| sev | file | effort | desc | date |' plus an empty 5-cell row) instead of one 6-column row — observable in TECHNICAL_DEBT.md and `tech-debt.sh list` output.

Line 40 is recommendation prose inside the usage() heredoc on stderr; severity is explicitly free-form and never validated or branched on anywhere in tech-debt.sh, so the dropped 'medium |' alternates no behavior at any consumer surface.

Flipping -lt to -le makes mark_escalation_if_capped return early at ITER==MAX_ITERATIONS(3), so the cap-hit Stop neither adds the qa-escalated label nor posts the J21 comment and the block reason lacks the ESCALATED wording — a third-iteration test asserting label or reason text distinguishes it.

At exactly ITER==3 the original appends the j21_options_block (the choose approve/continue/tech-debt/defer menu) to the FAILED_CHECKS block reason while the -gt mutant omits it until iteration 4, an observable difference in the hook's stdout reason that drives the agent's escalation choice.

With -ge 0 the no-match case (matched_line_number=0) takes the merge branch, so a brand-new lesson is never appended to LESSONS.md (awk's NR==0 rewrites nothing) and the stdout JSON reports action merged with entry_line 0 instead of action appended.

The mutant removes a never-added 'qa-escalate' label so the real qa-escalated (added by verify-before-stop line 786) survives enter/approve/choose, observable via bd show labels and the Stop hook's task_has_label replay path that keeps treating the task as escalated after `choose continue`.

remove_rubric_pending stops removing the rubric-pending label that cmd_enter adds, so after enter then approve the task keeps rubric-pending and `qa-gate.sh status` reports rubric=pending instead of rubric=none — a deterministic divergence on the status subcommand's stdout JSON and bd label state.

Line 219 is descriptive prose inside the usage() heredoc printed to stderr on argument errors; no code or consumer parses or branches on this help text, so the typo'd label name changes no observable behavior.

Double-incrementing line_number makes matched_line_number twice the real line, so the merge path's awk rewrites the wrong ledger line (or none) and the stdout JSON's entry_line field reports 2k instead of k even on the idempotent noop path.

bump_iteration's value is written to the counter file, printed into every block reason ('iteration N of 3'), and compared against MAX_ITERATIONS, so the very first Stop reports iteration 2 and reaches the cap/escalation a fire early — directly observable in the hook's reason text and counter file.

This printf is a builtin emitting the fingerprint into cmd_set's command-substitution capture (fp=$(compute_repo_fingerprint)), a write that cannot fail in any constructible test, so the masked return code is never observed and the captured value is byte-identical.

Line 225 is the usage() heredoc's description of the choose continue effect, stderr-only help prose that no script, label operation, or consumer branches on — the actual label name used by remove_escalation_labels (line 177) is untouched.
